title: On the Sovereignty of Duty
author: From: Florence Nightingale
content: I attribute my success to this—I never gave or took any excuse. The world is full of people explaining why things couldn't be done; be the one who does them.

title: The Art of Precision
author: From: The Victorian Era Reformers
content: A standard is not a suggestion; it is the floor upon which the next stage of development is built. If the foundation is loose, the structure will eventually fail.

title: The Survivorship Bias
author: From: Abraham Wald
content: We only look at the successes and ignore the failures. To truly understand a system, you must study the 'holes' in the planes that didn't return, not just the ones that did.

title: The Mere-Exposure Effect
author: From: Robert Zajonc
content: We develop a preference for things merely because we are familiar with them. Don't mistake 'familiarity' with 'superiority'.

title: The Power of Inaction
author: From: Rolf Dobelli
content: In complex environments, taking action for the sake of 'doing something' is often a liability. Sometimes the most strategic move is to wait for the system to clarify itself.
